hypothesis: Mutating position 5 to aromatic residues (e.g., W, F, Y) will enhance complementarity with SSTR2-specific ECL2/TM5 residues, while mutating position 11 to hydrophobic residues (e.g., L, I, V) will reduce off-target SSTR3/5 binding by avoiding unfavorable electrostatic interactions. This will increase the selectivity margin over native SST-14.

critic_feedback_used.hypothesis: The current design strategy is effective in generating high-affinity binders but insufficient in achieving SSTR2 selectivity. A focused mutational strategy at non-pharmacophore positions is necessary to disrupt conserved interactions with off-target SSTRs.

proposed_changes.rationale: To improve selectivity, we need to disrupt conserved contacts in the SSTR3/SSTR5 binding pockets. Focusing design efforts on non-pharmacophore positions will allow us to break off-target interactions while preserving the pharmacophore (FWKT(7-10) + Cys3/Cys14).
These mutations are designed to break conserved hydrogen bonds and hydrophobic interactions in SSTR3/SSTR5 pockets while maintaining flexibility and compatibility with the SSTR2 pharmacophore.
